short_title: live sprint-state assertions gate every PR on base drift
integrity_anchor: stories/sprint-state.yaml (spec_process tier) — committed factory-artifacts tip violated its own PC3 def-b ordering invariant (E-21 rows appended, not insert-sorted)
rationale: Rubric: 'closes a real defect class with a load-bearing test' — identical failure on all ten open PRs with local reproduction, both root causes named (data violation at factory-artifacts 27c37d11 + live-artifact tests silently becoming PR-blocking gates over data no PR can modify, against their own PORTABILITY design notes). RESOLVED-FULL 2026-07-21 by maintainer close: Fix B merged as PR #725 (8f17eea1) and Fix A landed as factory-artifacts 3781a0d8 re-sort. Priority rung assigned this run (issue was filed and closed between run-14 and run-15 enumerates).
quick_win_disqualification: integrity=true (sprint-state.yaml source-of-truth invariant violation) — HARD EXCLUSION; also already resolved
cited_evidence: CI run 29760563515 job 88413844881 (PR #714) + same single failure on all ten; factory-artifacts 27c37d11 ordinals 159-163; PORTABILITY notes sprint-state-format.bats lines 44-77, 938. Resolution verified: PR #725 merged 2026-07-21T14:04Z, maintainer close comment cites factory-artifacts 3781a0d8 canonical re-sort; closed COMPLETED.

short_title: lock renew still repo-relative in state-burst/state-manager
rationale: Rubric: 'closes a real defect class with a load-bearing test' — completes merged PR #526's own conversion (the #472 failure mode): state-burst SKILL.md lines 124/176/203 and state-manager.md line 239 still invoke factory-lock-write.sh/factory-cas-push.sh repo-relative, so in installed-plugin (marketplace-tarball) context the lock-RENEW remediation path itself cannot resolve. Filed by arcaven (measured human-review identity) with exact line cites; fix is the identical mechanical change #526 already made, plus an optional bats assertion on the ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} form (nothing currently gates the path form).
cited_evidence: Sites verified in checkout at e9f56d73: plugins/vsdd-factory/skills/state-burst/SKILL.md:124,176,203; agents/state-manager.md:239. Refs #472 (failure mode), #526 (merged conversion this completes), #242 (dispatcher-side class).

short_title: adversary compile-state clause unsatisfiable for read-only agent
rationale: Rubric: '(self-referential) a pilot-derived engine process-gap ... hardens the factory's own gates' — the #532-added compile-state clause (adversary.md ~191-193) asks the adversary to observe build/test state while its Tool Access section (~367) denies Bash/exec/process. The failure direction is the concern: combined with 'not reportable without a verification citation', it may invite dropping a legitimate CRITICAL/HIGH 'X is not exported' finding on an assumed-but-unobservable build-pass — a false-CLEAN edge on exactly the class #532 hardens, the same execution-predicated inference mode #627 documents. Two prose-only reconciliation shapes offered (orchestrator-fed corroboration, or #627's severity-cap). No observed instance yet; graded on the structural contradiction, which is verifiable in-repo.
quick_win_disqualification: two reconciliation shapes need a maintainer design choice (orchestrator-fed corroboration vs severity-cap); not a single obvious mechanical edit
cited_evidence: Clause-vs-tool-access contradiction cited at adversary.md ~191-193 vs ~367; filed by arcaven (measured human-review identity). Refs #532 (the hardening this edges), #627 (execution-predicated inference false verdicts), #465 (the false-BLOCKER class #532 closed).

short_title: author-absolute /Users/jmagady paths in shipped skill examples
rationale: Rubric: 'closes a real defect class with a load-bearing test' — author-environment absolute paths (/Users/jmagady/Dev/corverax/.reference/...) baked into instruction templates in brownfield-ingest, disposition-pass, and codebase-analyzer; an agent imitating them on any other machine targets a nonexistent directory. Same author-environment-leak class as #229, path flavor. Proposes the class-closing guard (no /Users/ literal in skills/ or agents/ prose) in tests/skills-content.bats. In-thread scope correction (arcaven, 2026-07-22): codebase-analyzer.md alone carries 13 occurrences (lines 21-35, 70-92), not 2 — fix surface larger than the instance list; a separate baked-artifact-filename class (6 corverax filename hits) is set aside and not yet filed anywhere.
cited_evidence: grep -rn '/Users/jmagady' plugins/vsdd-factory/ reproduces at develop 6444ac23 (still present at e9f56d73). Instances: brownfield-ingest/SKILL.md:101-102,197; steps/step-c-convergence-deepening.md:66; disposition-pass/SKILL.md:53-54; codebase-analyzer.md:21-22 (+11 more per thread). Related #229 (frontmatter flavor), #736 (its fix PR).

short_title: develop tracks a .lazyclaude worktree gitlink — git 128 on every CI job
rationale: Rubric: 'tightens an existing gate / removes a drift surface' — a mode-160000 gitlink at .lazyclaude/worktrees/<slug> (no .gitmodules entry) swept into the CODE branch by a 2026-06-20 PreCompact flush that also wrote to the wrong branch; warns exit-128 on every CI job's checkout post-step (red-herring risk when triaging genuinely red jobs — how it was found) and breaks --recurse-submodules clones. Proposes rm --cached + .gitignore, a flush-guard refusing unintended gitlinks, and a repo-health mode-160000 check. In-thread correction (arcaven, 2026-07-22): 82163b7f exists as a develop ancestor (2026-06-01, #168), so the gitlink is self-referential rather than dangling — impact unchanged since .gitmodules still has no url; cross-links #263 as the same accidental-gitlink class from the consumer-repo direction, both covered by the proposed ls-files -s mode-160000 check.
cited_evidence: git ls-tree -r develop .lazyclaude reproduces (mode 160000 -> 82163b7f); git submodule foreach fatal reproduced locally; CI warning on run 29775420903 (PR #725) post-checkout step. Introduced by 997c8c1e (PreCompact flush committed to code branch). Related #341/#515 (leak family — index-entry variant content scans cannot see), #263 (same class, consumer direction).

short_title: pr-manager step-9 cleanup verified by narration, not output
rationale: Rubric: pilot-derived engine process-gap hardening the factory's own gates — observed on switchboard-blue PR #128 (2026-07-21): pr-manager reported 'remote branch deleted via ls-remote exit 2' while both the remote branch AND the local worktree still existed; caught only by independent post-merge disk verification. In-thread root-cause correction (arcaven, 2026-07-22): shipped step-8c prose already uses the correct git ls-remote --exit-code form with an exact-line stdout match requirement, and the issue's proposed [ -z ... --heads ] form would regress that; what survives is sharper — the agent reported an exit code that command cannot produce on that ref (verification narrated but never executed), and step 9 (pr-manager.md:389) inherits step 8's conclusion with no verification requirement of its own for worktree/local-branch removal. The surviving ask: output-asserting re-list verification mandated at step 9.
quick_win_disqualification: the issue's own proposed fix was corrected in-thread (would reintroduce prefix-matching the shipped check defeats); surviving fix needs the narrated-verification framing, not a mechanical swap
cited_evidence: Observed switchboard-blue PR #128 2026-07-21 (step_complete transcript vs ls-remote ground truth). Thread verification (arcaven): agents/pr-manager.md:309-334 step-8c uses --exit-code + exact-line match; :389 step 9 carries no verification spec. Class: agent-reports-completed-action-it-did-not-complete.

short_title: post-convergence production edits merge without gate re-entry
integrity_anchor: per-story convergence seal (BC-5.39.001) — 3-CLEAN verdict certifies a frozen code HEAD that is not the HEAD that merges; nothing binds the seal to shipped code
rationale: Rubric: 'improves traceability or convergence soundness' + pilot-derived — observed on switchboard-blue PR #128: story converged 3/3 at frozen HEAD 5c8db39, then PR-phase CI-fix commits (one touching production code: relay_rate_cap.go +39 lines test-injection seam) merged with no gate re-entry; safety rested on manual orchestrator inspection, not a pipeline gate. The convergence verdict silently ceases to describe the code that ships — the #313 false-certification family at story scope. In-thread remedy note (arcaven, 2026-07-22): the PR layer already has the needed shape — BC-5.42.001 check-stale-verdict.sh records covered_sha and halts merge on HEAD movement; ConvergenceState carries no equivalent, so a converged_sha field mirroring covered_sha is a cheaper path than commit classification and avoids a test-only exemption in an unconditional gate.
cited_evidence: Observed: switchboard-blue S-BL.DISCOVERY-WIRE PR #128, converged HEAD 5c8db39, production commit cmd/switchboard/relay_rate_cap.go (+39, newRelayRateCapWithMax/capLimit seam). Thread: BC-5.42.001 covered_sha precedent (pr-manager.md Step 8-pre-A); ConvergenceState = passes_clean/last_finding_count/last_classification only.

rationale: Rubric: '(self-referential) a pilot-derived engine process-gap ... that hardens the factory's own gates/convergence — on-mission, not self-reference.' The adversary's Semantic Anchoring Audit (adversary.md ~line 214) checks that traceability-table row descriptions match the target artifact's title but has no call-path-vs-grep ground-truth dimension; the finding cites five consecutive coherence-lens passes that verified a benignly-correct Conformant flag while a factually-wrong 'class-1 production-reachable' description survived (symbol had zero production callers).
quick_win_disqualification: defect_nature is design-architectural — adding a description-accuracy audit dimension to the coherence lens requires judgment about what ground-truth cross-check to require, not a mechanical or pure-docs edit
cited_evidence: Verified adversary.md Semantic Anchoring Audit ('Do traceability-table row descriptions match the target artifact's actual title?', ~line 214) and Coherence Review axes (lines 249-280) are title/index-sync focused; no call-path-vs-grep dimension. Confirms the named gap.

short_title: version-qualifier prose drift invisible to input-hash detection
rationale: Rubric: 'tightens an existing gate / removes a drift surface' + pilot-derived self-referential engine process-gap. validate-input-hash.sh hashes file CONTENTS, so a stale version-qualifier string (e.g. 'SS-07 v4.13' after SS-07 advanced to v4.14) embedded as a current-version claim in dependent prose is invisible to drift tooling; two sibling instances cited in one convergence run. Not integrity: the stale cites do surface as adversarial findings on a later pass (signal exists), just not proactively.
quick_win_disqualification: defect_nature design-architectural — the fix requires a version-citation-coherence check that distinguishes live current-version claims from frozen provenance/changelog anchors (a new annotation convention), not a mechanical edit
cited_evidence: Verified plugins/vsdd-factory/hooks/validate-input-hash.sh hashes file contents via compute-input-hash; spec-versioning SKILL records a changelog but has no cross-artifact version-qualifier propagation check. Confirms input-hash would not catch prose version-string drift.

short_title: errata cited as normative authority but no resolvable artifact
integrity_anchor: .factory/ decision log / adjudication + convergence-history (learning tier) — a normative authority cited by ID with no dereferenceable artifact
rationale: Rubric: 'improves traceability or convergence soundness' + self-referential pilot-derived engine gap. A second erratum recorded only as changelog rows + commit narrative is cited by ID as normative authority by a behavioral contract, a story, and three contracts, yet 'no grep, no directory listing, no graph walk can dereference it' — the issue states the failure mode 'is identical to fabricated-anchor citation (the anchor-integrity family), except the authority DID exist as an event, not an artifact.' This makes the traceability/adjudication system-of-record disagree with reality by construction. Not silent-data-loss: content is recoverable from changelog rows and a fresh-context reviewer caught it (signal raised).
quick_win_disqualification: integrity=true (anchor-integrity: sibling artifacts cite a normative authority that is unresolvable by construction) — HARD EXCLUSION
cited_evidence: No adjudications directory in the develop checkout (.factory/ holds only logs/, matching the manifest note that spec_process/learning tiers are most exposed to produced-but-not-committed loss); adversary.md carries an 'Intent adjudication rule' (line 353) but no first-class-erratum-artifact requirement. Issue cross-refs #605/#671/#667/#327 (anchor-resolution family).

short_title: convergence gate misses reverse symbol->anchor walk
rationale: Rubric: 'tightens an existing gate / removes a drift surface' + pilot-derived self-referential engine gap. The convergence gate walks from declared ACs/BCs to tests but never from the production symbol table back to a spec anchor, so newly-exported symbols added during rework (KeystrokeSink, WithKeystrokeSink, AccessNode.Sweep, ConsoleSet.Heartbeat/EvictStale — cited with zero tests/callers/anchors) are invisible: 'neither required nor forbidden.' Not integrity: the gate does its designed job; this is a coverage gap that allows public-surface drift, not corruption of an engine system-of-record.
quick_win_disqualification: defect_nature design-architectural — adding a reverse symbol-enumeration convergence-gate axis with an explicit intentionally-unanchored escape hatch requires design judgment, not a mechanical edit
cited_evidence: convergence-check SKILL is 7-dimension; traceability-extension SKILL confirms the chain anchors on declared L1<->L4 / story / AC / BC artifacts with no production-symbol-table->spec reverse walk. Cited symbols are downstream product code (S-3.02, rc.22), not present in this engine checkout — pilot-derived from an external run.

rationale: Rubric: pilot-derived engine process-gap that hardens the factory's own gates — explicitly NOT the 'SPECULATIVE self-referential process-gap with no observed failure' drift case, because it cites a concrete failure: AC-008's only test calls Sweep(-time.Second) (negative duration forces every comparison true) so it cannot demonstrate the selective-eviction property AC-008 actually requires, and fanout_test.go uses EvictStale(-time.Second) for the same reason — green, correctly named, BC-traced, yet never runs the production arithmetic. The AC<->test fidelity axis catches this only via human inspection today.
quick_win_disqualification: proposal for new review-axis machinery (input-range classification + required inside-range companion test); defect_nature design-architectural, requires design, not a mechanical/docs edit
cited_evidence: Cited tests (AC-008 Sweep(-time.Second), fanout_test.go EvictStale(-time.Second)) are downstream product code from S-3.02 pass-4, rc.22 — pilot-derived, not verifiable in this engine checkout. AC<->test fidelity axis exists in the convergence/adversary machinery per convergence-check + adversary.md.

rationale: Rubric: pilot-derived engine process-gap hardening the adversary's review coverage — cites a concrete resource leak: ConsoleSet.Remove (graceful path via Detach) closes only entry.downstream while ConsoleSet.EvictStale (crash/stale path via Sweep) closes both downstream AND upstream, so a caller draining entry.upstream leaks the goroutine on graceful Detach. Each method reads as correct in isolation; the defect exists only in the asymmetry between teardown sets, invisible to per-method review. Sibling of #752 (both S-3.02 pass-4). Factory liveness unaffected (product-code leak, not a pipeline halt).
quick_win_disqualification: proposal for new review-axis machinery (per-type resource-ownership enumeration + teardown-set diffing across teardown methods); requires design, not a mechanical edit
cited_evidence: Cited symbols (ConsoleSet.Remove/EvictStale, Detach/Sweep, entry.downstream/upstream) are downstream product code from S-3.02 pass-4, rc.22 — not present in this engine checkout; pilot-derived. Sibling proposal to #752.
